title: Borscht
description: A traditional Eastern European beetroot soup made with beef stock, vegetables and soured cream.
context: There are numerous versions of borscht as it is a speciality of Eastern European countries, such as Russia, Lithuania, Poland and the Ukraine. It always contains beetroot, but here the traditional fermented beetroot liquid is replaced with a splash of vinegar.
steps: Heat the oil in a large, deep pan and gently fry the onion, leek, carrot and celery with a large pinch of salt for about 20 minutes until softened.
Stir in the beetroot, potato and tomato purée. Cook for 1 minute.
Pour in the stock and vinegar and season well. Bring to the boil, then cover and simmer for 45–60 minutes until the beetroot is tender. Leave to cool.
Blitz in batches in a blender or food processor until smooth. Return to the rinsed-out pan and stir in two-thirds of the cream. Adjust the consistency with a little extra stock if necessary.
Reheat without boiling and adjust seasoning if necessary. Serve hot or chilled with an extra swirl of cream in warmed bowls.
